content: The intrinsic reward signal generated by an ICM curiosity mechanism can be repurposed to prioritize the retention of new memories in 2025-state-of-the-art active learning agents, creating an autonomous 'curiosity gate' that only stores experiences the agent perceives as novel or unpredictable, rather than retaining all interaction data. This gated memory architecture lets curious autonomous agents scale to long-horizon open-ended tasks without suffering from catastrophic forgetting of high-value surprising experiences or memory bloat from redundant predictable interactions.
